alternate_forms: neiamg -- I look in on him/her briefly -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular animate object)
neiamul -- I look in on you briefly -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)
neiamit -- He/she looks in on me briefly -- (third person singular animate subject, first person singular animate object)

usages.translation: Tetuji mesgi'g pugtew lpa neiamgleg ta'n wigieg.
usages.english: The fire was so big, it ablazed near my home.

usages.translation: Lpa telta'siap neiamglet nitap, teligsamu'gl na utapsunn.
usages.english:
I thought that my friend appeared in a blaze, his/her clothes are so
bright.

alternate_forms: neiamglejig -- They appear burning -- (third person dual animate)
neiamgla'tijig -- They appear burning -- (third person plural animate)

usages.english: From his/her pocket he/she made a card appear.

alternate_forms: neiamugwa'l'g -- I make or cause him/her to appear -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular animate object)

usages.english: What did he/she cause to appear on the floor?

alternate_forms: neiamugwa'tu -- I make or cause it to appear -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)
neiamugwa'tueg -- We make or cause it to appear -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

usages.english: He/she only checked on supper and he/she left.

alternate_forms: neiaptm -- I check on it briefly -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)
neiaptmeg -- We check on it briefly -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

definitions: step out from behind somewhere in full view

usages.english:
Everytime he/she thought he/she had left him/her behind, he/she
steps into view.

alternate_forms: neiapugua'si -- I step into view -- (first person singular animate)
neiapugua'tieg -- We step into view -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
neiapuguita'ieg -- We step into view -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

definitions: come into view with a light source

usages.translation: We'gwiawisiw neiaseniget na na'gu'set, tetujigaqi'sg winigisg'g.
usages.english:
The sun seldom shines through, there's been so much inclement
weather.
Every Friday they go to have a drink at Taps.

alternate_forms: nemgwei -- I drink -- (first person singular animate)
nemgweieg -- We drink -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
nemgutieg -- We drink -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english:
The little boy looked very surprised, when he saw a moose for the
first time.

alternate_forms: nemi'g -- I see him/her -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular animate object)
nemu'l -- I see you -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)
nemi'lit -- He/she sees me -- (third person singular animate subject, first person singular animate object)

definitions: grandfather (term of respect for old man)

usages.english: Grampa who are we related to?

usages.translation:
Negm nemisg'g, gatu ugsampugwelg na wigumugsitesnu aq ginu
elg apoqonmuatesnu.
usages.english:
He/she is going to get it, but if it's too much then he/she will call for
us and we will help him/her.

alternate_forms: nemisgm -- I get it -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)
nemisgmeg -- We get it -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

usages.english: I've come here to get you.

alternate_forms: nemisgaq -- I get him/her -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular animate object)
nemisgul -- I get you -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)
nemisguit -- He/she gets me -- (third person singular animate subject, first person singular animate object)

alternate_forms: nemitegei -- I see -- (first person singular animate)
nemitegeieg -- We see -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
nemitega'tieg -- We see -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Ta'n tujiw nmitu'n awti nigtua's'g, ugjipenugewei ugsua'tu.
usages.english: When you see the road split, take the one going east.

alternate_forms: nemitu -- I see it -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

usages.english:
Last night my wife and I were at the wake all night because we

definitions: come running out of the woods
comes running out into the open

definitions: horse drawn winter sleigh on runners

Mu assma geitug ta'n telialteja'laj, gatu pemeiwatl, giseiwatal na
glapis.
usages.english:
He/she doesn't really know how to operate it, but he/she is figuring
it out (by operating it), he/she will be able to do it properly at
some point in the future.

alternate_forms: pemeiaq -- I am operating it (a tractor) -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular animate object)

translations: He/she is passing by and laughing

usages.english: Pierre passed by laughing a while ago.

alternate_forms: pemelmi -- I am passing by and laughing -- (first person singular animate)
pemelmieg -- We are passing by and laughing -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemelmultieg -- We are passing by and laughing -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Gisipgwatoq pmg'tew gesgmnaq ula newtipunqeg gaqianug.
usages.english:
If he/she can help it, he/she will have passed it before the end of
this year.

alternate_forms: pemgm -- I pass it -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)
pemgmeg -- We pass it -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

usages.translation: Gesigawigwet na ula Ji'me'j, gi's ugsisl pemguatl ta'n telpitoqsinij.
usages.english:
James is growing fast, he's already passed the hight of his older
brother.

alternate_forms: pemgaq -- I go past him/her -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular animate object)
pemgul -- I go past you -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)
pemguit -- He/she goes past me -- (third person singular animate subject, first person singular animate object)

usages.translation: Gina'sit pemgwi't'g na gwitn muta maqatgwig.
usages.english:
He/she has to work hard to steer that canoe because of the big
waves.

alternate_forms: pemgwi'tm -- I am steering it along -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)
pemgwi'tmeg -- We are steering it along -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

usages.translation: Te's nemi'g Pa'tlig na pemi'pit, etna amuj gsatmutug ali'pij.
usages.english:
Each time I see Patrick he's running along, he must like running
around.

alternate_forms: pemi'pi -- I am running along -- (first person singular animate)
pemi'pieg -- We are running along -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemi'pultieg -- We are running along -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Sitmug pemi'pugwet So'sep, ta'n tami ugjietug?
usages.english:
Joseph is staggering along the shore, where could he have come
from?

alternate_forms: pemi'pugwei -- I am staggering along -- (first person singular animate)
pemi'pugweieg -- We are staggering along -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemi'pugwa'tieg -- We are staggering along -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: The jocky is riding along the road.

alternate_forms: pemi'puluei -- I am riding along on (horse)back -- (first person singular animate)
pemi'pulueieg -- We are riding along on (horse)back -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemi'pulua'tieg -- We are riding along on (horse)back -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: It goes by every day at five.

alternate_forms: pemiaqal -- They are going by -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english: Your girlfriend is going by.

alternate_forms: pemiei -- I am going by -- (first person singular animate)
pema'tieg -- We are going by -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemitaieg -- We are going by -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: He/she tries to go fast, but can't help it, he/she goes along slowly.

alternate_forms: pemigegwi'pi -- I am running along slowly -- (first person singular animate)
pemigegwi'pieg -- We are running along slowly -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemigegwi'pultieg -- We are running along slowly -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Wejgwateja'lut ala San-Pie'l aqq utepaqan pemigp'ta'q.
usages.english: John-Peter is driving towards us and his car is blowing smoking.

alternate_forms: pemigp'ta'ql -- those are smoking along -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.translation: Ula maljewe'j pemiet teliawanigtmat, pas'g pemigp'ta't.
usages.english:
The youngster walking by smokes so awkwardly, he/she is just
making smoke.

translations: He/she is moving or walking along the shore

usages.english: I saw Pierre going along the shore this morning.

alternate_forms: pemijajiga'si -- I am  walking along the shore -- (first person singular animate)
pemijajiga'tieg -- We are walking along the shore -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemijaji'getaieg -- We are walking along the shore -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

translations: He/she is paddling along the shore

usages.english: Frank is paddling along that small lake.

alternate_forms: pemijajigisugwi -- I am paddling along the shore -- (first person singular animate)
pemijajigisugwieg -- We are paddling along the shore -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemijajigisugultieg -- We are paddling along the shore -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

translations: He/she is paddling or rowing along

usages.english: They will be paddling along for a week.

alternate_forms: pemiji'mei -- I paddling along -- (first person singular animate)
pemiji'meieg -- We are paddling along -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemiji'm'tieg -- We are paddling along -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Nemitu pemijuig, gatu mu geitu ta'n elijuig.
usages.english: I see it flowing along, but I don't know where it's flowing to.

alternate_forms: pemijuigl -- They are flowing along -- (third person plural inanimate)

translations: He/she is becoming larger and rounder

usages.english: These berries are really getting big and round, let's pick them.

alternate_forms: pemimqapsg'si -- I am becoming large and round -- (first person singular animate)
pemimqapsg'sieg -- We are becoming large and round -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemimqapsg'sultieg -- We are becoming large and round -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

translations: It is becoming larger and rounder

usages.english: My bread is becoming large and round, it's rising well.

alternate_forms: pemimqapsgegl -- They are becoming larger and rounder -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english: He/she is going along cycling and singing.

alternate_forms: pemintu -- I am going along singing -- (first person singular animate)
pemintueg -- We are going along singing -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemintu'tieg -- We are going along singing -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

translations: It is sliding or coasting along

usages.translation: Pemipqamig na tepaqan aq mu' wen teppigw.
usages.english: The sled is sliding along and nobody is onboard.

alternate_forms: pemipqamigl -- They are sliding along -- (third person plural inanimate)

translations: He/she is coasting or skating along

usages.translation: Pemipqamianeg telaqtmulnap npitn gatu mu nemi'wt'p.
usages.english: When I went skating by I waved at you but you didn't see me.

alternate_forms: pemipqami -- I am sliding by -- (first person singular animate)
pemipqamieg -- We are sliding by -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemipqamultieg -- We are sliding by -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: Let me help you as you stand up, it is so slippery here.

alternate_forms: pemiqama'si -- I am standing up -- (first person singular animate)
pemqama'tieg -- We are standing up -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemqamita'ieg -- We are standing up -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Pemisiawa'sit na ula gitapinu aq mu naqa'sigw.
usages.english: Our friend is passing by and not stopping.

alternate_forms: pemisiawa'si -- I am passing by -- (first person singular animate)
pemisiawa'tieg -- We are passing by -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemisiawita'ieg -- We are passing by -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: S\he just went sailing by, s\he was supposed to pick me up.

alternate_forms: pemisiawaqtegei -- I am sailing along by -- (first person singular animate)
pemisiawaqtegeieg -- We are sailing along by -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemisiawaqtega'tieg -- We are sailing along by -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

definitions: pass by while continuing to cry
translations: He/she is passing by while continuing to cry

usages.english: That same little girl is going by crying again.

alternate_forms: pemisiawtemi -- I am passing by and crying -- (first person singular animate)
pemisiawtemieg -- We are passing by and crying -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemisiawtemultieg -- We are passing by and crying -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

translations: He/she is running up the hill

usages.english: The runner runs up and down the mountain everyday.

alternate_forms: pemisqoqitugwi'm -- I am running up the hill -- (first person singular animate)
pemisqoqitugwi'meg -- We are running up the hill -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemisqoqitugwi'mu'tieg -- We are running up the hill -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: As I paddled by I saw them arrive at the beach.
I wonder how many years this river has been flowing by.

alternate_forms: pemitgl -- They are flowing -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english: It's getting cold again.

usages.translation:
Gesitlo'tm pemitgiaq, pe' epsaqtejg najiangamg'p, etugjel pugtew
peminqasua's'g.
usages.english:
I find it is getting chilly, I'll go check the stove, maybe the fire is
dying out.

alternate_forms: pemitgiaql -- They are getting chilly -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english: Cover that child, he/she is getting cold.

alternate_forms: pemitgiei -- I am getting cold -- (first person singular animate)
pemitgieieg -- We are getting cold -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemitgia'tieg -- We are getting cold -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Je gi's pemtma's'g mgumi aq pas'g nige' Apignajit.
usages.english: The ice is already breaking up and it's only February.

alternate_forms: pemitma'tigl -- They are starting to break -- (third person dual inanimate)
pemitmita'ql -- They are starting to break -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english: Be careful, the one you are using is starting to break off.
Now that's the fourth one I've seen runing up the stairs.

alternate_forms: pemitqoju'tugwi'm -- I am running upstairs -- (first person singular animate)
pemitqoju'tugwi'meg -- We are running upstairs -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemitqoju'tugwi'mu'tieg -- We are running upstairs -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: Joseph is whistling along the road.

alternate_forms: pemiwigwa'smugwei -- I am whistling along -- (first person singular animate)
pemiwigwa'smugweieg -- We are whistling along -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemiwigwa'smugwa'tieg -- We are whistling along -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: See that frog hopping along?

alternate_forms: pemja'i -- I leap along -- (first person singular animate)
pemja'ieg -- We leap along -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemjoltieg -- We leap along -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

translations: He/she is paddling or rowing him/her along

usages.english: Mary is rowing her friend along

alternate_forms: pemji'malg -- I rowing him/her along -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular animate object)
pemji'malul -- I rowing you along -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)
pemji'malit -- He/she is rowing me along -- (third person singular animate subject, first person singular animate object)

translations: He/she is paddling or rowing it along

usages.translation: Malia'n aq Sapatis pemji'matmi'tij gwitn, pi'tawa'tijig.
usages.english:
Marian and Jean Baptiste are paddling the canoe along on their
way up the coast.

alternate_forms: pemji'matm -- I rowing it along -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)
pemji'matmeg -- We are rowing it along -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

translations: He/she is paddling or rowing along

usages.english: S\he is going fast, paddling along by her\himself.

alternate_forms: pemji'mei -- I am paddling along -- (first person singular animate)
pemji'meieg -- We are paddling along -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemji'm'tieg -- We are paddling along -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Gi's ap peml'ga't na wijei ji'nm.
usages.english: That same man  is walking along again.

alternate_forms: peml'gai -- I am walking along -- (first person singular animate)
peml'ga'ieg -- We are walking along -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
peml'goltieg -- We are walking along -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Ulagu gisa'tuap aq poqtlugweg'p, aq gisgug me' pemlugweg.
usages.english:
I fixed it yesterday and it began working, and today it's still
working along.

alternate_forms: pemlugwegl -- those go along working -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english:
I saw him/her working along the road, he/she was trimming small
trees.

alternate_forms: pemlugwei -- I go along working -- (first person singular animate)
pemlugweieg -- we go along working -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemlugutieg -- we go along working -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

translations: He/she is carrying him/her on the shoulder

usages.english:
What is wrong with your dog, why are you carrying your dog on
your shoulders.

alternate_forms: pemn'galg -- I am carrying him/her on the shoulder -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular animate object)
pemn'galul -- I am carrying you on my shoulder -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)
pemn'galit -- He/she is carrying me on his/her shoulder -- (third person singular animate subject, first person singular animate object)

translations: He/she is carrying it on his/her shoulder

usages.translation: Ge' apoqnmu ala ji'nm mpo'qon pemn'gatg.
usages.english: Would you help that man carrying a bed on his shoulder.

alternate_forms: pemn'gatm -- I am carrying it on my shoulder -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)
pemn'gatmeg -- We are carrying it on our shoulder -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

usages.english: Frank is going up the mountain with a shoulder pack.

alternate_forms: pemn'gei -- I am going by with a shoulder pack -- (first person singular animate)
pemn'geieg -- We are going by with a shoulder pack -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemn'ga'tieg -- We are going by with a shoulder pack -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation:
Pana ne'gaw na tele'g, ma na newte'gtnug ta'qwei pemo'tg
newtsini'w.
usages.english:
He/she has always been like that, he/she will always be doing
more than one thing at a time.

alternate_forms: pemo'tm -- I am operating it -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)
pemo'tmeg -- We are operating it -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

usages.translation: Pempa'q na ula, me' glu'gt'tew jaqaliasoqoma'ti'gw.
usages.english: The tide is rising, it would be better if we go across fast.

translations: He/she is in the process of sitting down

usages.translation: Ala nemi't pempa'sit, na negm getu'glult.
usages.english:
See that person over there about to sit down, that is the one you
want to speak to.

pempa'tieg -- We are in the process of sitting down -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pempita'ieg -- We are in the process of sitting down -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Pempega's'g me' na ula ntul, amuj na espeg.
usages.english: The water is still rising in my boat, there must be a leak.

alternate_forms: pempegitgl -- They flow along -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english:
I saw him\her down the shore a while ago towing his\her boat
along.

alternate_forms: pempilm -- I am towing it along -- (first person singular animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)
pempilmeg -- We are towing it along -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

usages.translation: Pas'g ne'w ajiet aq gi's pem'pisgiaq.
usages.english: It's just four o'clck and it's already dusk

definitions: start to recover from bad times
translations: He/she is starting to get on his/her feet

usages.english: As s/he starts to get her/his footing, s/he begins to slip again.

pem'pugua'tieg -- We are starting to get on our feet -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pem'pugueta'ieg -- We are starting to get on our feet -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Ge' angam ala giwnig, pemqo'get aq nespatalg.
usages.english: Look at that otter, s/he is floating along while eating.

alternate_forms: pemqo'gwei -- I am floating along -- (first person singular animate)
pemqo'gweieg -- We are floating along -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemqo'gwa'tieg -- We are floating along -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

alternate_forms: pemqo'qwegl -- They are floating along -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english: Your papers are flying by here.

alternate_forms: pems'gl -- They pass by in the air -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english: Why is there a wall from here to beside the door?

alternate_forms: pemsaqas'gl -- They are partitioned -- (third person plural inanimate)

alternate_forms: pemsiawqo'qwegl -- pem'siawqo'qwekl -- They are floating by -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english: He/she is floating by, let him/her be.

alternate_forms: pemsiawqo'qwei -- I am floating by -- (first person singular animate)
pemsiawqo'qweieg -- We are floating by -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemsiawqo'qwa'tieg -- We are floating by -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english:
Every time I see him/her, he/she is hurrying on his/her way to
somewhere else.

alternate_forms: pemsin -- I am hurrying by -- (first person singular animate)

pemsultieg -- We are hurrying by -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Pemsulatl ugti'l, mu gejiaq ta'n eliej.
usages.english:
He/she is hurrying by with his/her dog, I don't know where he/she
is going.

pemsulul -- I am hurrying by you -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)

pemsutmann -- I am hurrying by with those -- (first person singular animate subject, third person plural inanimate object)

usages.translation: Gi's pana ula San Pie'l utepaqan pemta'q.
usages.english: As usual here's John Peter's car going by making noise.

alternate_forms: pemta'ql -- They go by making noise -- (third person plural inanimate)

usages.english: Did you see that snake slithering along?

translations: He/she is going by and crying

alternate_forms: pemtemi -- I am going by and crying -- (first person singular animate)
pemtemieg -- We are going by and crying -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemtemultieg -- We are going by and crying -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english:
Sean passed his house a long time ago while he was looking
around.

pemtesgmeg -- We are kicking it along -- (first person plural exclusive animate subject, third person singular inanimate object)

pemtesgul -- I am kicking you along -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)
pemtesguit -- He/she/it is kicking me along -- (third person singular animate subject, first person singular animate object)

usages.translation:
Gi's geji'g'pnig pmto'gutitaq, ma' na telmaljewe'jg
gistlipgitapulti'gw.
usages.english:
I knew they would fall asleep, when they are that young they aren't
able to stay up that late.

pemto'gweieg -- We drift off to sleep -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemto'gutieg -- We drift off to sleep -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

The little boy is prattling along, he's so happy to be walking

pemtoqsieg -- We are gabbing by -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemtoqsultieg -- We are gabbing by -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

alternate_forms: pemtugwi'm -- pem'tukwi'm -- I am running along -- (first person singular animate)
pemtugwi'meg -- pem'tukwi'mek -- We are running along -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemtugwi'mu'tieg -- pem'tukwi'mu'tiek -- We are running along -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

translations: He/she is going with something tucked under the arm

Algimiet aq pemtulgomit ula pilei mila'suaqan, getu najimila'sit na
ala witapal wiguaq.
usages.english:
He/she is sneaking about and going along with the new toy tucked

house.

pemtulgomieg -- We are going with something tucked under the arm -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemtulgomultieg -- We are going with something tucked under the arm -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: My friend is slowly recrovering, he/she had been so sick.

alternate_forms: pemulo'tegei -- I am getting progressively better from illness -- (first person singular animate)
pemulo'tegeieg -- we are getting progressively better from illness -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pemulo'taqatieg -- we are getting progressively better from illness -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.english: That's a huge egg she just layed.
April is the Easter month.

usages.translation: Nti na smtug pene'sugwa'tew nmi'sg goqwei etlmagutmn.
usages.english: My dog will immediately drool if he/she sees you eating anything.

alternate_forms: pene'sugwa'i -- I drool -- (first person singular animate)
pene'sugwa'ieg -- We drool -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pene'sugwa'tieg -- We drool -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

usages.translation: Pene'sugwati waju'pet ala mijua'ji'j, amuj na wipitl wespaiatl.
usages.english:
That child is wet with drool, his\her teeth must be bothering
him\her.
Our cow gave birth to a calf this morning.

usages.english:
That guy you see over there will not have a good life, because he
looks down on everyone.

penoqwite'lmul -- I look down on you -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)
penoqwite'lmit -- be/she looks down on me -- (third person singular animate subject, first person singular animate object)

usages.english: Sew these buttons on here.

usages.translation:
Nipitl elugwatmuimg'pnn aq sa'qatia'limg'p, me' newte'
telpissagutnai.

I had dental work done and I was given a needle, my mouth is still
numb from it.

pissieieg -- We are becoming numb -- (first person dual exclusive animate)
pissia'tieg -- We are becoming numb -- (first person plural exclusive animate)

definitions: sop up (e.g., gravy, molasses) with bread
translations: He/she sops up gravy, molasses etc. with bread

usages.english:

definitions: bread spread or sop (fat, molasses, etc.)

alternate_forms: pistamunn -- dips -- (plural)

pisuimul -- I slander you -- (first person singular animate subject, second person singular animate object)

usages.translation: Pisuiw pegisin, gaqa mes'tesgmaneg ta'n te's'g goqwei.

alternate_forms: pita'ql -- They are long -- (third person plural inanimate)

definitions: tethered (by long rope, string, chain, twine)

usages.translation: Nti mesgilg aq amujpa pittaqpisit gujmug.

My dog is large and he/she has to be tethered by a long rope

I'm afraid that this rope might be a bit too long.

tqoqwejl -- a wild cat -- (fourth person)

usages.translation: Apjiw gjijiaten gigjiw ig, muta nutuaten alwigwa'smugwet.
